steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content:
The roots are $x = 7$ and $x = -2$, so $f(x) = a(x-7)(x+2)$ for some integer $a \gt 1$. Expanding: $f(x) = ax^2 + a(2-7)x - 14a = ax^2 - 5ax - 14a$. This means $b = -5a$, so:
$$a + b = a + (-5a) = -4a$$
We need a value of $-4a$ where $a$ is an integer greater than 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "-8": $-4a = -8 \implies a = 2$. Integer greater than 1. ✓
  • "-4": $-4a = -4 \implies a = 1$. Not greater than 1. ✗
  • "6": $-4a = 6 \implies a = -\frac{3}{2}$. Not a positive integer. ✗
  • "7": $-4a = 7 \implies a = -\frac{7}{4}$. Not a positive integer. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
